Let me implement Core Data model XML. Xcode uses specific format for relationships.

```xml
<entity name="CategoryEntity" representedClassName="CategoryEntity" syncable="YES" codeGenerationType="class">
    <attribute name="color" optional="YES" attributeType="String"/>
    <attribute name="id" optional="YES" attributeType="UUID" usesScalarValueType="NO"/>
    <attribute name="name" optional="YES" attributeType="String"/>
</entity>
<entity name="SubtaskEntity" representedClassName="SubtaskEntity" syncable="YES" codeGenerationType="class">
    <attribute name="id" optional="YES" attributeType="UUID" usesScalarValueType="NO"/>
    <attribute name="isCompleted" optional="YES" attributeType="Boolean" usesScalarValueType="YES"/>
    <attribute name="title" optional="YES" attributeType="String"/>
    <relationship name="task" optional="YES" maxCount="1" deletionRule="Nullify" destinationEntity="TaskEntity" inverseName="subtasks" inverseEntity="TaskEntity"/>
</entity>
```

TaskEntity needs:
```xml
<attribute name="categoryId" optional="YES" attributeType="UUID" usesScalarValueType="NO"/>
<relationship name="subtasks" optional="YES" toMany="YES" deletionRule="Cascade" destinationEntity="SubtaskEntity" inverseName="task" inverseEntity="SubtaskEntity"/>
```

```xml
    <entity name="CategoryEntity" representedClassName="CategoryEntity" syncable="YES" codeGenerationType="class">

    <entity name="SubtaskEntity" representedClassName="SubtaskEntity" syncable="YES" codeGenerationType="class">

        <attribute name="isCompleted" optional="YES" attributeType="Boolean" usesScalarValueType="YES"/>
        <attribute name="title" optional="YES" attributeType="String"/>
        <relationship name="task" optional="YES" maxCount="1" deletionRule="Nullify" destinationEntity="TaskEntity" inverseName="subtasks" inverseEntity="TaskEntity"/>

    <entity name="TaskEntity" representedClassName="TaskEntity" syncable="YES" codeGenerationType="class">

    </entity>
</model>
```

```swift
func toggleComplete(id: UUID) {
    guard var task = tasks.first(where: { $0.id == id }) else { return }
    let subCount = (try? subtaskRepository.fetchSubtasks(taskId: id))?.count ?? 0
    if subCount > 0 {
        let newCompleted = !task.isCompleted
        try? subtaskRepository.setAllSubtasksCompleted(taskId: id, completed: newCompleted)
        task.isCompleted = newCompleted
    } else {
        task.isCompleted.toggle()
    }
    ...
}
```

```swift
private func syncParentCompletion() throws {
```

```swift
func toggleCompletion() throws {
    let subs = try subtaskRepository.fetchSubtasks(taskId: task.id)
    if subs.isEmpty {
        var t = task
        t.isCompleted.toggle()
        try repository.save(t)
        task = t
    } else {
        let newVal = !task.isCompleted
        try subtaskRepository.setAllSubtasksCompleted(taskId: task.id, completed: newVal)
        var t = task
        t.isCompleted = newVal
        try repository.save(t)
        task = t
    }
}
```

Wait - if subs non-empty and newVal is true, we set all subtasks complete and parent complete. Good.
If newVal is false, set all incomplete. Good.

```swift
}
```

```swift
var selectedCategoryId: UUID? // nil means all categories
private(set) var categories: [Category] = []

func load() {
```

toggleComplete with subtasks:
```swift
func toggleComplete(id: UUID) {
    guard var task = tasks.first(where: { $0.id == id }) else { return }
    do {
        let subs = try subtaskRepository.fetchSubtasks(taskId: id)
        if subs.isEmpty {
            task.isCompleted.toggle()
        } else {
            let newCompleted = !task.isCompleted
            try subtaskRepository.setAllSubtasksCompleted(taskId: id, completed: newCompleted)
            task.isCompleted = newCompleted
        }
        try repository.save(task)
        load()
    } catch {}
}
```

```swift
private func applyTask() {

}
```

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what was implemented, aligned with your instruction doc and the project’s **Core Data + MVVM** conventions (persistence is in Core Data, not in-memory).

### Data & persistence
- **`Category`** (`Models/Category.swift`), **`Subtask`** (`Models/Subtask.swift`), **`Task.categoryId`** (`Models/Task.swift`).
- **Core Data**: `CategoryEntity`, `SubtaskEntity` (optional `task` → `TaskEntity`), `TaskEntity.categoryId` + `subtasks` cascade delete.
- **Mapping**: `CategoryEntity+Mapping.swift`, `SubtaskEntity+Mapping.swift`, updates in `TaskEntity+Mapping.swift`.
- **`CategoryRepository`** / **`SubtaskRepository`** with protocols; `SubtaskRepository` includes `setAllSubtasksCompleted(taskId:completed:)` for list/detail completion when subtasks exist.
- **`StorageService`**: lightweight migration flags so the new model can load existing stores.

### UI
- **`CategoryPickerView`**: “Category” label + `UIPickerView` (“None” + categories); wired on **create/edit task**.
- **`SubtaskCell`**: checkbox-style button + title.
- **`CategoryListViewController`** + **`CategoryListViewModel`**: list, **+** to add (alert), swipe to delete; deleting a category clears `categoryId` on tasks via the repository.

### Task list
- Header: **folder** (categories CRUD), **tag** (filter by category), existing **filter** (status), **+**.
- **`TaskListViewModel`**: `selectedCategoryId`, `categories` loaded with tasks; list cells show **category name** when set.

### Task detail
- **`SubtaskViewModel`**: load / add / toggle / delete; when there is **at least one subtask**, parent **`isCompleted` follows “all subtasks completed”**; with **no** subtasks, parent completion is unchanged after deletes (per edge-case note).
- **`TaskDetailViewModel`**: if the task **has subtasks**, **Mark complete / Mark as active** flips **all subtasks** to match (same idea as the list checkbox).
- Category line when `categoryId` resolves.

### Accessibility
- New IDs under **`AccessibilityIDs`** for category list, category filter alert, task cell category, task form picker, task detail subtasks.

`xcodebuild` for **generic iOS Simulator** completed with **BUILD SUCCEEDED**. If an old store ever fails to migrate, delete the app from the simulator once and reinstall.

**Files to browse**: `Repository/CategoryRepository.swift`, `Repository/SubtaskRepository.swift`, `ViewControllers/CategoryList/CategoryListViewController.swift`, `ViewControllers/TaskDetail/TaskDetailViewController.swift`, `SceneDelegate.swift` (wires the three repositories into the list screen).

---

CreateTaskViewController:
```swift
private let categoryRow = CategorySelectionRowView()

categoryRow.onTap = { [weak self] in
    self?.presentCategoryPicker()
}

private func presentCategoryPicker() {
    viewModel.loadCategories()
    let picker = CategorySelectViewController(
        categories: viewModel.categories,
        selectedCategoryId: viewModel.categoryId
    ) { [weak self] id in
        self?.viewModel.categoryId = id
        self?.refreshCategoryRow()
    }
    navigationController?.pushViewController(picker, animated: true)
}

private func refreshCategoryRow() {
    categoryRow.configure(categories: viewModel.categories, selectedCategoryId: viewModel.categoryId)
}
```

```swift
func tableView(_ tableView: UITableView, didSelectRowAt indexPath: IndexPath) {
    tableView.deselectRow(at: indexPath, animated: true)
    let id: UUID? = indexPath.row == 0 ? nil : categories[indexPath.row - 1].id
    onPicked(id)
    navigationController?.popViewController(animated: true)
}
```

CategorySelectionRowView:
```swift
final class CategorySelectionRowView: UIView {
    var onTap: (() -> Void)?
    private let headingLabel = UILabel()
    private let tapContainer = UIControl() // or UIButton
    private let valueLabel = UILabel()
    private let chevron = UIImageView(image: UIImage(systemName: "chevron.right"))

    func configure(categories: [Category], selectedCategoryId: UUID?) {
        if let id = selectedCategoryId, let name = categories.first(where: { $0.id == id })?.name {
            valueLabel.text = name
        } else {
            valueLabel.text = String(localized: "None")
        }
    }
}
```

tapped -> onTap?()

Here’s what changed:

### 1. `CategorySelectionRowView` (replaces `CategoryPickerView`)
- Subheadline **“Category”** and a **tappable row** (rounded background, current value + chevron).
- Tap runs `onTap` so the form can push the next screen.
- `configure(categories:selectedCategoryId:)` updates the value label (`None` or category name) and `accessibilityValue`.

### 2. `CategorySelectViewController` (`ViewControllers/CategorySelect/`)
- `AppHeaderView` + **Back**.
- **`UITableView`** (inset grouped): **None** first, then each category.
- **Checkmark** on the current selection.
- Tapping a row calls `onPicked(UUID?)`, then **pops** the navigation stack.

### 3. `CreateTaskViewController`
- Uses `categorySelectionRow` instead of a picker.
- `presentCategorySelect()` pushes `CategorySelectViewController` and, on pick, sets `viewModel.categoryId` and calls `refreshCategoryRow()`.
- Save still uses `viewModel.categoryId` (updated when the user chooses on the new screen).

### 4. `AccessibilityIDs`
- `CreateTask.categorySelectionRow` for the row control.
- New `CategorySelect` identifiers for the picker screen.

`CategoryPickerView.swift` was removed; the project builds successfully.
